text: So from what I know about that, the most models are usually in black, are considered the black box. So the popular techniques that I've used before are not using knowledge graph, using symbolic AI to somewhat make the models predictions interpretable and ground them using actual facts. This is one one using symbolic AI in the process itself. Other ways include models like machinery models like Shaq and Lyme, which are inherently interpretable. But if in that in the case you're asking of where the models predictions are not easily interpretable at all, maybe I would, if I'm using a leglem, for example, I would ask it for a chain of thought, or something along those lines where I can allow the model to be able to tell me where it got the idea from.
